title: veronica serpyllifolia var. langei
intro: Veronica serpyllifolia var. langei, also known as Creeping Speedwell, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant that belongs to the balm family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Veronica serpyllifolia var. langei include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, or topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
